etymology.description: Unknown origin: possibly related to 元(元) (yuán, 'origin'), 天(天) (tiān, 'heaven'), or 堯(堯) (yáo, 'a legendary emperor; tall')
etymology.interpretation: The character's origin remains undetermined, but it shares usage with 無(無) (wú, 'not have; without') and was historically used interchangeably with it to represent the concept of non-existence or absence.
etymology.simplification: Simplified to 无 by streamlining strokes and adopting an alternate simple form for ease of writing and recognition.

examples.chinese: 他无话可说(他無話可說)。
城市的夜空几乎无星(城市的夜空幾乎無星)，只有月亮(只有月亮)。
examples.pinyin: Chéngshì de yèkōng jīhū wú xīng, zhǐ yǒu yuèliàng.
examples.english: The city's night sky is almost without stars, only the moon.

examples.chinese: 他们之间无解(他們之間無解)，只能随缘(只能隨緣)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zhījiān wú jiě, zhǐ néng suíyuán.
examples.english: There's no solution between them, they can only go with the flow.
